faqs.question: Are yellow jackets dangerous to my New Springville home and family?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are aggressive and can sting multiple times, posing serious risks especially to children, pets, and anyone with allergies. Unlike honeybees, yellow jackets can become highly defensive of their nests, which are often built in wall voids, attics, or underground near New Springville homes. Professional removal is the safest option.

faqs.question: How do I prevent yellow jackets from returning to my New Springville property?
faqs.answer: After removal, we seal entry points and treat areas where yellow jackets commonly nest. We recommend keeping garbage cans sealed, removing fallen fruit from yards, and scheduling periodic inspections during peak season (late summer/early fall). Our technicians provide customized prevention tips based on your specific New Springville property layout.
